name: Apricot Brandy Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the dried apricots and brandy; cover and refrigerate overnight.
2. Cream the shortening, gradually add sugar and beat well.
3. Add egg and mix well.
4. In a separate bowl, combine one cup of flour with baking soda, spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, cloves), and salt; set aside.
5. Combine pecans with the apricot mixture.
6. Add the reserved flour mixture to the shortening and sugar mixture, alternating with applesauce, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Beat well.
7. Stir in the remaining cup of flour into the shortening and sugar mixture.
8. Pour the batter into a greased 9 x 5 x 3 inch loaf pan.
9. Bake at 350°F for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Cool the loaf in the pan for 10 minutes, then remove and cool completely on a wire rack.
11. Yield: 1 loaf.
12. Notes: May be frozen up to 3 months. This is more of a sweet cake than a bread. May be wrapped in cheesecloth and soaked with additional brandy and stored in a tin, if desired.
